results.title: How to Track Your Brand in ChatGPT, Perplexity & Gemini | RankinLLM
results.content:
## Step 3: How to Track Your Brand Mentions in Perplexity

Perplexity works differently from ChatGPT in one important way: it cites its sources. When Perplexity mentions your brand, it shows which URLs it pulled from. That citation data is extremely valuable for understanding why you appear or why you do not.

Run the same query list from Step 1 at perplexity.ai. For each result, record:

 Whether your brand is mentioned
 Where it appears in the answer
 The sentiment: positive, neutral or negative
 Which URLs are cited alongside or instead of your brand [...] ## Step 2: How to Track Your Brand in ChatGPT

ChatGPT is the most widely used AI search tool and the first place to check. Here is how to run a manual brand check:

 Go to chat.openai.com and open a new conversation. Starting fresh matters because ChatGPT carries context from previous messages.
 Type your first query from the list you built in Step 1. Use natural language, the same way a real buyer would ask.
 Read the full response carefully. Note whether your brand is mentioned, where in the response it appears, what is said about it, and whether the description is accurate.
 Open a new conversation and repeat the same query. Do this five times for each query.
 Record your findings in your tracking log (see Step 5 for the exact format).

Tip [...] This is the reality for most brands right now. AI search tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity and Gemini have become a primary discovery channel for buyers researching products and services. Unlike Google, which shows a list of links, these tools synthesise an answer and deliver it in seconds. If your brand is not part of that answer, you are invisible at the exact moment a buyer is making a decision.

The problem is that most brands have no idea how they appear in AI results. They are not tracking it, not monitoring changes, and not responding when something goes wrong. This guide walks you through a practical, step-by-step process to track your brand in ChatGPT, Perplexity and Gemini.

## Why Tracking Your Brand in AI Search Matters

results.title: I cancelled my ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini subscriptions for Claude
results.content:
### It doesn’t make the same mistakes repeatedly

While it's definitely nice to have someone agree with everything you say, it quickly becomes frustrating when that "agreement" comes at the expense of accuracy or helpfulness. With ChatGPT in particular, you'll constantly come across users complaining about the fact that it feels overly flattering and glazes you even when it shouldn't. In fact, this problem got so bad with a new model update that OpenAI had to roll it back entirely. Gemini and Perplexity are less guilty of this, but they still lean toward telling you what you want to hear rather than what you need to hear.

results.title: I spent a few weeks scanning B2B brands in ChatGPT, Perplexity and Gemini. Most founders are checking their AI visibility completely wrong
results.content: Second, AI barely ever cites your own website. The domains that kept showing up across the most different brands were Wikipedia, G2, and Reddit. For one bootstrapped tool, 88% of the citations in its AI answers went to third-party pages and only 5% to its own domain. The AI recommends you, and someone else gets the click. Third, the three engines disagree constantly. A page Perplexity leans on often isn't touched by ChatGPT. Checking one and assuming the rest is a mistake. What I'd actually do about it: Audit discovery prompts, not your brand name. Write the 15 questions a stranger with your problem would type, and run them. Searching your own name tells you nothing useful, because you always show up for people who already know you. Get onto the third-party pages AI already cites in your [...] - the discovery prompt point is huge and i think a lot of teams are accidentally measuring brand recall instead of market visibility. asking chatgpt "what do you know about company x" feels reassuring, but buyers rarely search that way if they've never heard of you. one exercise we've started doing is collecting actual sales call language and turning those into problem based prompts. it was uncomfortable seeing how often competitors appeared instead of us, but it gave us a much clearer roadmap than another rank tracking report ever did. visibility before awareness feels like the real challenge now. [...] - This lines up with what I found. Most founders check once, type their brand name into ChatGPT, see it describe them and assume they are visible. That is the vanity check. The better test is the buyer query without your name in it, ask it to recommend tools or agencies in your category and see if you come up at all, because that is what your prospects are actually typing. I took ShowUpWithAI's free audit to get a proper read on this, and the gap they showed me was brutal, perfect on anything with my brand name, completely invisible on every category question. They walked through why, basically no third-party citations, inconsistent entity data, and content the models could not easily quote. None of it was a quick fix, it builds over months. If you want a cleaner read yourself, run twenty

## The ChatGPT Visibility Playbook

Audit your current ChatGPT visibility across the queries your buyers actually ask, build distributed citations across the platforms ChatGPT trusts, publish original data and comparison content, densify review profiles, and track weekly. Read the Claude companion piece and the Perplexity companion piece for the full picture.

results.title: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini or Perplexity: which is the best ...
results.content:
## How to choose the priority AI to monitor for your brand

Tracking all 4 LLMs from day one is rarely relevant or budget-justified. An effective monitoring strategy in 2026 prioritizes the AIs that match your business target. Here is the prioritization grid I apply with my Cockpyt AI clients.

If your target is B2C mainstream: start with ChatGPT and Gemini. These are the 2 AIs with the largest user volume, integrated into mainstream ecosystems (iOS, Android, Microsoft, Google). Perplexity and Claude come in the second wave once the base is covered. [...] For a brand, ChatGPT represents the largest possible citation surface but also the least traceable. The model tends to paraphrase without naming sources, except when SearchGPT is explicitly activated. This opacity makes citation tracking more complex than on Perplexity, which systematizes clickable links.

ChatGPT’s main strengths for a brand:

 Unmatched user volume that maximizes exposure potential
 Plugin and custom GPT ecosystem with agent features (Operator) opening AAO
 Native Microsoft 365 integration via Copilot, reaching B2B enterprise users
 Multimodal generation (text, images, video, audio) broadening use cases

Limitations for brand citation: [...] For a brand, Claude is interesting for three reasons. It cites sources more traceably than ChatGPT, its hallucination rate is lower according to 2026 comparisons, and its 200,000-token context window allows long document analysis without losing the thread. For YMYL sectors (health, finance, legal), this rigor matters.

Brand limitations:

 No image generation (vs ChatGPT and Gemini)
 Limited web access depending on version, gradually integrated in 2026
 User volume still below ChatGPT and Gemini
 Smaller integration ecosystem, despite progressive opening (Slack, Chrome, Excel, MCP)

Brand verdict: better-quality citations but smaller audience. To monitor as priority if your target is B2B, technical, or if factual rigor is central to your sector.
